::: {.r-fit-text}
## 🎯 Goal: Understand and Handle Table Parameters
:::


::: {.cell}

:::



# What are Table Parameters?

## Concept

- Table parameters in [OSPS](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-mobi/mobi-documentation/parameters-formulas-tags#table-formulas)

- Parameters can be defined as Tables made up out of pairs of x and y values.

- The parameter value will be linearly interpolated between the specified values. 

. . .

:::columns
:::{.column width="50%"}
Examples of parameters defined by a table:

- Enzyme ontogeny
- Compound solubility
:::

:::{.column width="50%"}

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](A11-Table-Parameters_files/figure-revealjs/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){fig-align='center' width=2100}
:::
:::

:::
:::

## Load an Example


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ospsuite)
# Load a simulation
sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "simple.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))

# Get the parameter defined by a table.
tableParam <- getParameter("Organism|TableParameter", sim)
```
:::


# Access Table Values

## Inspect the Parameter


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam <- getParameter("Organism|TableParameter", sim)
print(tableParam)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|TableParameter
  • Value: 1.00 [1/min]

── Formula ──

  • isTable: TRUE
  • XDimension: Time
  • UseDerivedValues: FALSE

── Table values ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  x= 0, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 10, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 30, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 40, y= 4, restartSolver= FALSE
  • Value overrides formula: FALSE
```


:::
:::

. . . 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$value
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 1
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$isTable
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] TRUE
```


:::
:::


## All points

Points are stored under the `allPoints` attribute of `formula` within a parameter.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$formula$allPoints
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[[1]]
  x= 0, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE

[[2]]
  x= 10, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE

[[3]]
  x= 30, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE

[[4]]
  x= 40, y= 4, restartSolver= FALSE
```


:::
:::


. . .

:::{.callout-note}
`allPoints` is a list of `ValuePoint` objects. Each has `$x`, `$y`, and `$restartSolver` fields.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
pt <- tableParam$formula$allPoints[[1]]
list(x = pt$x, y = pt$y, restartSolver = pt$restartSolver)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$x
[1] 0

$y
[1] 1

$restartSolver
[1] FALSE
```


:::
:::

:::

## Specific points


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$formula$valueAt(xValue = 10)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 2
```


:::
:::

. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$formula$valueAt(xValue = 30)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 3
```


:::
:::


## Linear Interpolation

`valueAt()` linearly interpolates between defined points.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$formula$valueAt(xValue = 20)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 2.5
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$formula$valueAt(xValue = 35)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 3.5
```


:::
:::


# Modify Table Values

## Override with a single value


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
setParameterValues(tableParam, 2)
tableParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|TableParameter
  • Value: 2.00 [1/min]

── Formula ──

  • isTable: TRUE
  • XDimension: Time
  • UseDerivedValues: FALSE

── Table values ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  x= 0, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 10, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 30, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 40, y= 4, restartSolver= FALSE
  • Value overrides formula: TRUE
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$isFixedValue
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] TRUE
```


:::
:::


## Reset to Original Values

`reset()` restores the table formula and clears the fixed-value override.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$reset()
tableParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|TableParameter
  • Value: 1.00 [1/min]

── Formula ──

  • isTable: TRUE
  • XDimension: Time
  • UseDerivedValues: FALSE

── Table values ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  x= 0, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 10, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 30, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 40, y= 4, restartSolver= FALSE
  • Value overrides formula: FALSE
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$isFixedValue
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] FALSE
```


:::
:::

## Add a new value 

You can add one or multiple points to the table 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
tableParam$formula$addPoints(5, 1.5)
tableParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|TableParameter
  • Value: 1.00 [1/min]

── Formula ──

  • isTable: TRUE
  • XDimension: Time
  • UseDerivedValues: FALSE

── Table values ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  x= 0, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 5, y= 1.5, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 10, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 30, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 40, y= 4, restartSolver= FALSE
  • Value overrides formula: FALSE
```


:::
:::


## Resources

-   [`{ospsuite}` vignette on table Parameters](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/table-parameters.html)

# Exercise

## Modify and Query a Table Parameter

1. Get the `Organism|TableParameter` from the simulation.
2. Check the value at `xValue = 25` (interpolated).
3. Override the table to a value of your choice 
4. Reset 



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
# 1)
tableParam <- getParameter("Organism|TableParameter", sim)

# 2)
tableParam$formula$valueAt(xValue = 25)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 2.75
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
# 3)
setParameterValues(tableParam, 2)

# 4)
tableParam$reset()
tableParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|TableParameter
  • Value: 1.00 [1/min]

── Formula ──

  • isTable: TRUE
  • XDimension: Time
  • UseDerivedValues: FALSE

── Table values ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  x= 0, y= 1, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 5, y= 1.5, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 10, y= 2, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 30, y= 3, restartSolver= FALSE
  x= 40, y= 4, restartSolver= FALSE
  • Value overrides formula: FALSE
```


:::
:::

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
